text:
Welcome to Fishplot Interactive Editor.

This guided tour will walk you step-by-step through the full workflow: loading data, editing clones, simulating drug effects, and visualizing clonal evolution.

Click Next to begin.

Start here: load a JSON data file.

The file must follow the Fishplot structured format including:
• patient_id
• sample_id
• node hierarchy
• mutation names
• clonal proportions

Select the patient you want to explore.

Each patient contains multiple timepoints describing clonal evolution over time.

Select a timepoint.

All editing actions (mutation editing, deletion, percentage updates) apply to the selected timepoint.

This button allows you to select drug effects:

• Select a drug from the list
• Apply its effect to the current timepoint

Drug effects are defined in the JSON file and automatically applied respecting clonal hierarchy.

This button allows you to edit existing clones:

• Edit mutation names
• Update clonal proportions
• Delete mutations (with automatic rebalancing)

All edits maintain biological consistency.

These buttons allow you to create new biological events:

• Add a new mutation (child clone)
• Create a new timepoint
• Simulate drug impact

The clonal hierarchy is always biologically constrained.

These buttons allow you to delete a mutation or an entire timepoint.

Deletion automatically rebalances clonal proportions and maintains hierarchical constraints.

This button allows you to add a new drug effect to the dataset.

Define the drug name, target mutation, and effect strength.

Drug effects are applied automatically during simulation.

Use these visualization buttons to switch between:

• Fishplot view
• Mutation tree view
• Raw data table
• Computed matrix

The Fishplot represents clonal evolution longitudinally.

Drug effects are applied between timepoints and automatically rebalanced to respect hierarchical constraints.

The Mutation Tree view shows the biological parent-child structure.

You can click directly on nodes to edit clone percentages.

The Data Matrix shows the final computed matrix after:

• Drug effects
• Zero reappearance correction
• Hierarchical constraints enforcement

You can edit metadata fields here.

This allows integration of clinical annotations, sample information, or experimental context.

You can save the modified dataset as a JSON file.

Drug effects and metadata are preserved in the exported structure.

You are ready to explore and simulate clonal evolution.

Remember: biological constraints are always enforced automatically to maintain consistency.

Enjoy using Fishplot Interactive.
